old_claim.note: Leather Tanning provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The saddle is constructed from shaped, stuffed, and edge-stitched hides and is explicitly characterized as sophisticated leather and needlework.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and construction analysis describe two stuffed wing-shaped hides sewn along their edges and call the artifact masterful leather and needlework.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The surviving saddle is materially defined by cut, stuffed, and stitched hide components assembled through leather-working techniques.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Artifact construction directly requires leather working, whereas the study does not identify the chemical tanning recipe needed to justify the narrower old edge.

ontology_before: The saddle depended on a specific tanning method that its archaeological study did not establish.
ontology_after: The saddle requires the directly observed leather-cutting, stuffing, and stitching capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: animal_pack_saddles now requires leather_working and no longer directly depends on leather_tanning.
would_reject_if: The Yanghai saddle were shown to use no shaped or stitched hide components.
short_reason: Leather working is observed directly; a particular tanning process is not.
